personas.name: CLI User (Integrator)
personas.primary_jtbd: Bootstrap an existing repository into DevForge, run orchestration cycles against it, and inspect structured results — without understanding graph nodes or Protocol internals.
personas.success_looks_like: devforge init succeeds and writes .devforge/. devforge fixture game_project returns JSON with cycle_id + dispatches. devforge doctor shows at least one executor as 'ok'. devforge snapshot .devforge/devforge.snapshot.json completes a cycle without errors.
current_pain_points: No Getting Started tutorial in English that walks through init → snapshot → cycle in 5 minutes
README.md is in Chinese — English-speaking users have no primary entry point
No snapshot schema reference — first-time users don't know what fields are required in a snapshot JSON
devforge doctor output is structured JSON but no docs explain how to act on 'blocked' vs 'missing' status
No example showing how to recover from a partial cycle (e.g., executor failed mid-run) using persistence
evidence: docs/cli.md:1-80 — CLI surface exists but is reference-only, no narrative walkthrough
README.md:24-66 — Quick Start exists in Chinese only
tests/test_main_cli.py:12-20 — fixture command integration test shows expected JSON output shape

personas.name: Adapter Implementer (Extender)
personas.primary_jtbd: Wire a new AI CLI tool (e.g., aider, cursor, a custom script) into DevForge as a first-class executor, or tune per-project context pull budgets and LLM routing without modifying graph code.
personas.success_looks_like: Custom ExecutorAdapter satisfies Protocol. register_executor_adapter() call succeeds. Custom executor appears in devforge doctor checks. project_config.json with pull_policy_overrides is applied and visible in dispatch metadata.
current_pain_points: No 'Build Your First Executor Adapter' how-to guide — the only documentation is the Protocol definition in base.py
submit vs submit_request vs dispatch method chain is undocumented — implementers have to read BaseExecutorAdapter source to understand which methods to override
No documented testing recipe for custom adapters without live CLI tools
pull_policy_overrides schema in docs/project-config.md is incomplete — no examples of ref_patterns or budget fields
SubprocessTransport lifecycle (submit/poll/cancel) is not documented as the recommended base for new adapters

personas.name: Graph Modifier (Contributor)
personas.primary_jtbd: Add new meta-graph nodes, extend RuntimeState with new lifecycle fields, or replace planning decision chains (concept, planning, retry) while keeping the 256-test suite green.
personas.success_looks_like: New node composes with existing nodes via RuntimeState dataclass extension. MockLLMClient-based cycle tests pass for the new node. 256 existing tests remain green without live network calls.
current_pain_points: No contributor guide explaining the node composition pattern (RuntimeState mutation → return state contract)
LangGraph wiring in langgraph_builder.py is not documented — contributors must reverse-engineer the StateGraph edges to understand how to add a new node
META_GRAPH_NODES and NEXT_STEP_BY_EVENT in skeleton.py are not obviously the right place to register new nodes
No explanation of why LangGraph was chosen and what the bounded-statelessness contract means for node functions
PLAN.md phase descriptions are outdated (C002, C003) creating wrong maturity expectations for contributors
